name: Puentes de las Paredes y de los Seis Ojos — circular por Navacepeda de Tormes - Parque regional Sierra de Gredos
region_intro: Jogging around Navalperal De Tormes offers routes through the rugged landscapes of the Sierra de Gredos, characterized by mountain passes, river valleys, and high-altitude terrain. The region features a mix of challenging ascents and more moderate paths, often providing views of the Gredos mountain range. Runners can expect varied surfaces, from well-trodden trails to more remote, rocky sections. Best jogging routes around Navalperal De Tormes The most popular jogging route is Puerto de Candeleda and Morezón Loop from Plataforma de Gredos, a 8.4 miles (13.5 km) trail that takes about 2 hours 19 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is Puentes de las Paredes y de los Seis Ojos — circular por Navacepeda de Tormes - Parque regional Sierra de Gredos, a moderate 6.7 miles (10.8 km) path. This route explores river crossings and the natural park scenery. Local runners also love the View of Circo de Gredos – Laguna Grande de Gredos loop from La Plataforma, a 8.1 miles (13.1 km) trail leading through high mountain cirques, often completed in about 2 hours 18 minutes. Jogging around Navalperal De Tormes is defined by mountain trails, river valleys, and high-altitude landscapes, with options ranging from easy paths to more challenging climbs. The network offers options for different ability levels, including easy jogging routes and more demanding running trails. The routes in Navalperal De Tormes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 200 runners have used komoot to explore Navalperal De Tormes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Navalperal De Tormes?
A: There are over 20 running routes around Navalperal De Tormes, offering a variety of experiences through the Sierra de Gredos. These include options for different skill levels, from moderate paths to challenging mountain trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Navalperal De Tormes?
A: The region is characterized by rugged landscapes, mountain passes, and river valleys. Runners can expect varied surfaces, from well-trodden trails to more remote, rocky sections, often with significant elevation changes as you traverse the high-altitude terrain of the Sierra de Gredos.

Q: Are there easy jogging paths for beginners in Navalperal De Tormes?
A: Yes, Navalperal De Tormes offers a few easier jogging routes suitable for beginners. While many trails involve challenging ascents, there are at least 2 routes classified as easy, providing a gentler introduction to running in the area's beautiful scenery.

Q: Are there circular running routes in Navalperal De Tormes?
A: Absolutely. Many of the running trails in the area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1158272636/puentes-de-las-paredes-y-de-los-seis-ojos-circular-por-navacepeda-de-tormes-parque-regional-sierra-de-gredos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puentes de las Paredes y de los Seis Ojos — circular por Navacepeda de Tormes&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate loop exploring river crossings and the natural park.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints along the jogging trails?
A: The running routes often provide breathtaking views of the Gredos mountain range. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24534365" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Circo de Gredos – Laguna Grande de Gredos loop from La Plataforma&lt;/a&gt; offer stunning panoramas of the high mountain cirques. You can also find highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4095576" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morezón Peak&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/540292" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Candeleda&lt;/a&gt; which are known for their vistas.

Q: Are the running trails in Navalperal De Tormes suitable for dogs?
A: Generally, the natural parks and trails in the Sierra de Gredos region are dog-friendly, provided dogs are kept under control, especially due to the presence of livestock and wildlife. It's always best to check specific park regulations, but most trails are open to well-behaved dogs on a leash.

Q: What do other runners enjoy the most about jogging in Navalperal De Tormes?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. Runners often praise the stunning mountain scenery, the challenge of the high-altitude trails, and the sense of immersion in nature that the rugged landscapes provide.

Q: Are there any natural attractions or landmarks to see while running?
A: Yes, the region is rich in natural beauty. Along your runs, you might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5758520" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tormes River&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5754260" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de las Paredes&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3758639" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de los Barrerones&lt;/a&gt;. These natural monuments add to the scenic appeal of the trails.

Q: Can I find running routes that start near parking areas?
A: Many popular trailheads, such as the Plataforma de Gredos, which serves as a starting point for routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1157689719/puerto-de-candeleda-and-morezon-loop-from-plataforma-de-gredos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Candeleda and Morezón Loop&lt;/a&gt;, typically have designated parking facilities. It's advisable to check specific route details for parking information before heading out.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Navalperal De Tormes?
A: The best time for running generally depends on the specific trail and your tolerance for weather. Spring and early autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can be hot, especially at lower altitudes, while winter brings snow and ice to higher elevations, making some routes more challenging or inaccessible.

Q: Are there any less crowded running routes in the area?
A: While popular routes like those starting from Plataforma de Gredos can see more visitors, exploring some of the moderate trails or those slightly further from main access points can offer a more solitary experience. The sheer number of trails means you can often find quieter paths, especially on weekdays or outside peak season.

Q: Are there any huts or shelters along the running trails?
A: Yes, particularly in the higher mountain areas, you can find huts and refuges. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2641568" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Elola Refuge – Laguna Grande de Gredos&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4744395" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Grande Refuge&lt;/a&gt; are notable shelters that can be found along some of the more challenging routes, offering a place to rest or take cover.
tips: A place that is worth adding to a route that passes through this area. In the well of the walls is located near the town of Navacepeda de Tormes, in the Barbellido river in which you can swim surrounded by a spectacular landscape. There is also a small bar next to it where you can have a drink or eat.
In this part of the path, on both sides you can see the Sierra de Greos with the Río Tormes that collects the waters and to the north, the town of Navacepeda de Tormes.
Old Romanesque bridge that crosses the waters of Río Tormes, at the foot of the Central System and that collects its waters along this long valley.

waypoints.name: Views of Navacepeda de Tormes and the Tormes River
